# Work Packet ALB-016 — Plugin Packaging + Launcher Wiring + Doctor Manifest Check

## 1. TASK

Make the `albion` plugin real: a plugin manifest, launcher wiring that loads the plugin into default-mode sessions, and a doctor check that diffs shipped assets against the version manifest.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (replaces the `.gitkeep`).
- `bin/albion` — default mode passes `--plugin-dir <repo>/plugin` to claude; `--vanilla` must NOT.
- `bin/albion-doctor` — new `manifest` check.
- `tests/test_launcher.sh` — updated argv expectations (sanctioned edit).
- `tests/test_plugin_manifest.sh` — new structural test.

## 3. CONTEXT

- **Read first:** `bin/albion` (launcher structure: `resolve_charter`, `final_argv` assembly, mode handling), `bin/albion-doctor` (`record_check` registry — add the new check without reshaping it), `bin/albion-compile` (`--check` semantics), `manifest/albion-manifest.yaml` (documented subset; `skills`/`agents` lists), `docs/research/reports/06-claude-code-modern.md` plugin section, `CONVENTIONS.md`.
- **plugin.json:** minimal and valid — `name: "albion"`, `version: "0.1.0"`, `description`, `author`. Skills/agents/hooks are discovered by directory convention; do not enumerate them in plugin.json.
- **Launcher wiring:** in default mode only, insert `--plugin-dir` + the resolved absolute plugin path into `final_argv` (position: after the charter args, before the model args, so existing ordering assertions extend naturally). Resolve the plugin dir from the same symlink-safe repo root the charter uses. If the plugin dir is missing, warn on stderr (mirror `warn_without_charter` style) and continue without it. `--vanilla` and `--doctor` behavior unchanged.
- **Doctor `manifest` check:** PASS when (a) `bin/albion-compile --check` exits 0, (b) every `skills`/`agents` path listed in the manifest exists on disk, and (c) `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` parses as JSON with a non-empty `name` — one-line detail summarizing counts (e.g. `charter in sync; 5 skills, 5 agents, plugin.json ok`). FAIL names the first discrepancy. SKIP only if `bin/albion-compile` is missing. Works under `--offline` (no network involved).

## 4. MUST DO

- `tests/test_launcher.sh` updates: default-mode cases gain the two plugin args (argc and argv indices shift — update every affected assertion, and add assertions that `--plugin-dir` is present in default mode, absent in `--vanilla`, and that a missing plugin dir warns but still execs). Keep every existing behavioral guarantee tested; do not delete cases.
- `tests/test_plugin_manifest.sh`, wired into `bash tests/run.sh`: plugin.json exists, parses (python3), has non-empty `name`/`version`; `bin/albion-doctor --offline` output contains a `manifest` line reading PASS; corrupting a temp *copy* of plugin.json and pointing the check at it is NOT required — keep the test to the real tree plus the doctor-line assertion.
- Preserve the doctor's existing check order and `--offline` exit semantics.
- Follow `CONVENTIONS.md` portability rules; run the full suite yourself before finishing.

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT create marketplace metadata, installers, or version-bump tooling (M6's lane).
- Do NOT touch `plugin/skills/`, `plugin/agents/`, `plugin/hooks/`, `plugin/scripts/`, `manifest/`, or the charter.
- Do NOT change `bin/albion-compile`.
- Do NOT modify anything outside the five paths in EXPECTED OUTCOME.
- Do NOT run git state-changing commands.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `bash tests/run.sh` fully green including the new test; no existing guarantee dropped.
- [ ] `bin/albion --dry-run` (default) shows `--plugin-dir`; `--vanilla --dry-run` does not.
- [ ] `bin/albion-doctor --offline` shows `manifest` PASS with a factual one-line detail.
- [ ] CI-equivalent shellcheck clean; `git status` shows only the five allowed paths.
